category_title: Students with academic anxiety who use physical gestures to conceptualize mathematical terms
scenario_title: Solving negative exponent problems using physical gestures
minimal_context: Luke is working on a math problem with a negative exponent in the denominator during a small group tutoring session.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's look at this next problem on the paper. It has a negative exponent in the denominator. Go ahead and try to solve it.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I worked on this but I can't figure out how to solve it.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What do you think you should do next?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: When you switch right? (Speaks in an unsure, questioning way)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Can you explain what you mean?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Uh…The same thing, but twist, right? (TWISTING his hand back and forth in a circular motion with his palm facing toward Matt)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Let's look at this next problem on the paper. It has a negative exponent in the denominator. Go ahead and try to solve it.

STUDENT: I worked on this but I can't figure out how to solve it.

TUTOR: What do you think you should do next?

STUDENT: When you switch right? (Speaks in an unsure, questioning way)

TUTOR: Can you explain what you mean?

STUDENT: Uh…The same thing, but twist, right? (TWISTING his hand back and forth in a circular motion with his palm facing toward Matt)

task.content: Working on negative exponent problems, specifically dealing with a negative exponent in the denominator and multiplying by the reciprocal
task.learning_goal: Build understanding and ability to communicate about a new topic (reciprocals)

context.materials: Diagrams and equations displayed on paper as visual supports
context.social_setting: Small group tutoring session working with tutor Matt and peer Henry (who is also school-identified as a student with LD in math and reading)

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When he had worked independently on a problem but could not figure out how to solve it
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Asked Matt for help, spoke in an unsure, questioning way, and began TWISTING his hand back and forth in a circular motion with his palm facing toward Matt
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Struggled with academic anxiety and was especially self-conscious about appearing incompetent in front of his peers

behavior_patterns.condition: When trying to define or conceptualize mathematical terms like reciprocals and verbal language is insufficient or he is unsure
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Uses specific physical gestures, moving his hand in a TWISTING motion, while speaking in an unsure, questioning way
related_quotes: Uh…The same thing, but twist, right?
